objectives.title: Verify results and handle ambiguity safely

questions.prompt: Which instruction is the most reproducible?

options.text: Open notes.txt, add the date on a new final line, and save it.

questions.explanation: Option B names the target, action, location, and completion step.

questions.prompt: What should follow an action in a reliable procedure?
questions.explanation: An observable check confirms whether the action achieved its intended result.

questions.prompt: Which features make a procedure reproducible?
questions.explanation: Named targets, clear actions, and stable ordering support repeatability; hidden assumptions do not.

questions.prompt: Why should a repeatable task keep a stable step order?

options.text: So another person can reproduce the outcome.
So ambiguous terms become precise automatically.

questions.explanation: Stable ordering makes the same process easier for another person to repeat.

questions.prompt: Which actions are appropriate when an instruction is ambiguous?
questions.explanation: Pausing, clarifying, and confirming intent reduce the risk of acting on an unsafe assumption.
